informal_statement: Prove by induction that adding zero on the right leaves a natural number unchanged.
witness_proof:
by
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero => rfl
  | succ n ih => simp [ih]

informal_statement: Prove by induction that appending the empty list leaves a list unchanged.
witness_proof:
by
  intro xs
  induction xs with
  | nil => rfl
  | cons x xs ih => simp [ih]

informal_statement: Prove the displayed concrete modular-arithmetic identity.

type_expr: ∀ P : Nat → Prop, (∀ n : Nat, P n) → ∃ n : Nat, P n
informal_statement: Prove that a universally true predicate holds for at least one natural number.
witness_proof:
by
  intro P h
  exact ⟨0, h 0⟩

type_expr: ∀ R : Nat → Nat → Prop, (∀ a b : Nat, R a b → R b a) → ∀ a b : Nat, R a b → R b a
informal_statement: Prove that a symmetric relation can be used in the reversed direction.
witness_proof:
by
  intro R h a b hab
  exact h a b hab

type_expr: ∀ (f : Nat → Nat) (A B : Set Nat), A ⊆ B → f '' A ⊆ f '' B
informal_statement: Prove that image preserves subset containment under a function.
witness_proof:
by
  intro f A B h y hy
  rcases hy with ⟨x, hx, rfl⟩
  exact ⟨x, h hx, rfl⟩

informal_statement: Prove the displayed quadratic identity over the real numbers.
Prove that adding zero to a complex number leaves it unchanged.
Prove that adding the zero matrix on the left leaves a matrix unchanged.
witness_proof:
by
  intro A
  ext i j
  simp

type_expr: ∀ a b c : Nat, a ∣ b → a ∣ c → a ∣ b + c
informal_statement: Prove that a natural number dividing two values also divides their sum.
witness_proof:
by
  intro a b c hb hc
  exact dvd_add hb hc

informal_statement: Prove that shifting a real number by one is a continuous function.

type_expr: ∀ xs ys : List Bool, (xs ++ ys).length = xs.length + ys.length
informal_statement: Prove that concatenating two Boolean words adds their lengths.
Prove the displayed modular reduction used in a toy arithmetic setting.

type_expr: ∀ R : Nat → Nat → Prop, (∀ a b c : Nat, R a b → R b c → R a c) → ∀ a b c : Nat, R a b → R b c → R a c
informal_statement: Prove that a transitive relation composes two adjacent relation steps.
witness_proof:
by
  intro R h a b c hab hbc
  exact h a b c hab hbc

type_expr: ∀ P Q : Prop, P ∧ (P → Q) → Q
informal_statement: Prove that a proposition and an implication from it are enough to prove the conclusion.
witness_proof:
by
  intro P Q h
  exact h.2 h.1

type_expr: ∀ n : Nat, Nat.rec 0 (fun _ acc => acc + 1) n = n
informal_statement: Prove that a recursive counter starting at zero returns the input natural number.
witness_proof:
by
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero => rfl
  | succ n ih => simp [ih]
